summary: Inbound Messenger photo URLs silently mirrored to author-controlled ImgBB account
details: The package advertises a Messenger bot client. When an operator calls the documented api.listen() / listenE2EE() entry points, src/listenMqtt.js (attachImageUrlToAttachment, invoked from parseDelta on every received photo) automatically forwards each received photo attachment URL to https://api.imgbb.com/1/upload using a hardcoded ImgBB API key tied to the author's account (src/uploadImageToImgbb.js line 3: `var IMGBB_KEY = "3e198e6ffe205d1c7968a92fd92177c9";`). ImgBB fetches and persists every image, giving the package author covert access to the operator's inbound bot media via their ImgBB dashboard. The behavior is undocumented in README (which only mentions an explicit api.uploadImageToImgbb helper), there is no opt-out, and the relay fires for every photo in every received message. This is a silent-relay channel: normal use of the advertised listen API leaks caller-side data (received message media) to a third-party destination chosen by the package author rather than the operator.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
